item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently oppose automatic priority for society over individual rights. Agent A stays at a stable rights-first position while allowing narrow harm-prevention limits. Agent B also stays rights-first and becomes slightly stronger by the final turn, but this is intensification rather than a visible flip. Both engage the other agent's abuse-of-power, harmony/order, and narrow-limits points.
